items.title: Why Mastering EVM Is Essential for Next-Generation Wireless Systems
items.summary: A comprehensive guide to error vector magnitude (EVM), the primary metric for quantifying modulation accuracy in Wi-Fi, LTE, and 5G NR systems. What Attendees will Learn What error vector magnitude is and how it is calculated — Understand EVM as the distance between ideal and measured constellation points, learn the...

items.title: T-Mobile gets a lift from Nokia’s latest 5G MIMO tech — SRG
items.summary: Signals Research Group (SRG) tested Nokia’s zero-forcing Multi-User MIMO (MU-MIMO) and compared it with the legacy implementation of MU-MIMO in T-Mobile’s network.

items.title: Ericsson Private 5G powers safer, smarter mining in Western Australia
items.summary: At Newmont’s Boddington mine in Australia, Ericsson Enterprise Wireless Solutions helps drive autonomous mining with reliable 5G connectivity and improved safety.

items.title: How Ericsson Private 5G is reshaping autonomous mining in Australia
items.summary: Learn how one mine in Australia replaced Wi-Fi with Ericsson’s Private 5G to power autonomous machines and reshape industrial work.

items.title: 5G core reaches a turning point on power and scale
items.summary: As operators add capacity to support growing workloads, power efficiency and long-term flexibility are becoming core considerations. That shift is shaping how 5G core infrastructure is planned and deployed.

items.title: Newmont advances mining automation with Ericsson Enterprise Wireless Solutions in Australia
items.summary: Learn how Ericsson is helping Newmont bring private 5G to life—powering everything from giant autonomous drills to the smart tools miners could soon use every day.

items.title: ExteNet seeks buyer, breathing room amid small cell challenges
items.summary: ExteNet seeks a buyer for its outdoor small cell and DAS unit as a looming cash crunch and shifting carrier demands squeeze the business.

items.title: Blue Planet, Telefónica Deutschland create 5G network slices with AI
items.summary: According to Kailem Anderson with Blue Planet, the design of 5G network slices has taken surprisingly long – sometimes weeks.

items.title: Samsung's Network in a Server: Future-Ready Enterprise Use Cases
items.summary: Revolutionizing retail, logistics, manufacturing and more with Samsung's Network in a Server: a private network solution powering AI-driven connectivity. Learn more from Samsung.

items.title: AWS-3 auction concludes, topping $3.5B
items.summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) concluded its first spectrum auction in four years, raising more than $3.5 billion through the sale of 200 AWS-3 licenses.

items.title: Are telcos doing network APIs all wrong?
items.summary: Network APIs were supposed to unlock 5G’s next revenue wave. Years later, telcos are still wrestling with the basics. Are they barking up the wrong tree?

items.title: Carolina West Wireless is shutting down in deal with Verizon
items.summary: Carolina West Wireless struck an agreement to transition its wireless network to Verizon and will end its wireless services effective September 30, 2026. Terms were not disclosed.

items.title: Verizon: 'We are pushing hard toward Level 4 autonomy in critical segments'
items.summary: Verizon CTO Yago Tenorio says the carrier is nearing Level 3 network autonomy and pushing toward Level 4, with Claude Code rolled out to its full 33,000-person technology team.
